channel.title: R1V3R5 on R1V3R5: An imaginary journal about fake rivers
channel.description: Recent content in R1V3R5 on R1V3R5: An imaginary journal about fake rivers

item.title: Hyrulean Hydrology: The riddle of Lake Hylia in 'Zelda: A Link to the Past'
item.description:
Introduction #

In The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past (ALttP), Lake Hylia receives water from Zora’s River, but no downstream surface outlet is visible.[1]Zelda Wiki (2026). Lake Hylia That absence creates a hydrologic problem: if inflow persists, long-run water losses must also persist, or lake storage should rise over time[2]Brutsaert, Wilfried (2005). Hydrology: An Introduction and dissolved load should accumulate.

The contrast with the original Legend of Zelda (NES) makes this omission interesting. In the first game, Lake Hylia is drawn with both incoming and outgoing rivers.[1]Zelda Wiki (2026). Lake Hylia In ALttP, by comparison, inflow is mapped but a matching outflow is not. This raises two linked questions: (1) what process keeps water level approximately stable, and (2) what process prevents long-run dissolved-load accumulation.

item.title: First River Notes
item.description:
This is the first post in the Hugo scaffold. The header above is animated and reseeds at every completed loop.

Technical notes can now include inline math like $Q = A / v$ inside a paragraph without breaking the reading rhythm.

Block equations also render in the article column:

$$ R(x) = \int_0^L \frac{\partial h(x, t)}{\partial t} , dt $$

Distill-style inline citations look like this [1]Gregor, Karol; Danihelka, Ivo; Graves, Alex; Rezende, Danilo Jimenez; Wierstra, Daan (2015). DRAW: A recurrent neural network for image generation and can be repeated without changing numbering [1]Gregor, Karol; Danihelka, Ivo; Graves, Alex; Rezende, Danilo Jimenez; Wierstra, Daan (2015). DRAW: A recurrent neural network for image generation.

Initial draft published on 2026-04-17.

No factual corrections have been recorded yet. Open an issue in the repository for map or terminology corrections.

No factual corrections have been made as of 2026-04-14.

If you spot an issue, please open a ticket in the project repository.

item.title: About
item.description: R1V3R5 is still small by design. The intention is to keep the publishing system legible, let the format evolve in public, and publish pieces that treat imaginary rivers seriously enough to require their own editorial home.
